_metadata.description: 8-layer CAR-T design decision framework. Each layer provides structured biological reasoning that cascades into element selection.

D1_disease_biology._note: First decision: what category of disease determines the fundamental design strategy.

characteristics: high antigen density on malignant cells
accessible in blood and bone marrow
low physical barriers (no stroma)
low-to-moderate immunosuppressive TME
well-defined lineage-restricted antigens

design_impact.hinge: short (proximal epitopes sufficient for most targets)
design_impact.armor: usually not needed; standard 2G CAR sufficient
design_impact.homing: not needed (tumor cells circulate in blood/BM)
design_impact.persistence: 4-1BB for durability; CD28 for rapid response

sources.source_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma

characteristics: physical barriers (stroma, ECM, fibrosis)
highly immunosuppressive TME (TGFβ, adenosine, PD-L1, IDO, hypoxia)
antigen heterogeneity within tumor
poor T cell trafficking and infiltration
T cell exhaustion from chronic antigen exposure
hostile metabolic environment (low glucose, low O2, high lactate)

design_impact.homing: REQUIRED — CCR2b (CCL2 axis) and/or CXCR3 (CXCL9/10 axis)
design_impact.armor: REQUIRED — cytokine payload (IL-15, IL-18, IL-12) + checkpoint blocker (anti-PD-L1, anti-TIGIT)
design_impact.anti_exhaustion: CRITICAL — c-Jun OE is minimum; consider BATF, NR4A1-DN, DNMT3A-KO
design_impact.ecm_degradation: consider HPSE (heparanase) for dense stroma tumors
design_impact.tgfb_resistance: TGFβRII-DN or TGFβ-trap for TGFβ-dominant TME
design_impact.hinge: depends on target epitope membrane distance

references: Rafiq et al. Nat Rev Clin Oncol 2020

sources.source_title: Engineering strategies to overcome the current roadblocks in CAR T cell therapy
A Phase I Trial of Regional Mesothelin-Targeted CAR T-cell Therapy in Patients with Malignant Pleural Disease, in Combination with Pembrolizumab

characteristics: target antigens (CD33, CD123, FLT3, CD70) shared with normal hematopoietic stem cells
myeloablation risk if HSCs are depleted
accessible in blood/BM (liquid tumor properties)
complex differentiation hierarchy with leukemic stem cells

design_impact.safety_switch: MANDATORY — iCasp9 or RQR8 to allow HSC rescue
design_impact.logic_gate: strongly consider AND-gate (SynNotch) to spare HSC: require two antigens, only one present on HSC
design_impact.cell_chassis: NK cells preferred — inherent HSC sparing via KIR/KIR-L inhibitory signaling
design_impact.affinity_tuning: lower-affinity binders may spare low-density normal cells
design_impact.transient_expression: mRNA CAR to limit exposure window

sources.source_title: CD123 CAR T cells for the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome

characteristics: target is pathogenic B cells or plasma cells (not a tumor mass)
low 'tumor burden' equivalent (pathogenic cell frequency much lower)
goal is reset of immune tolerance, not complete cell elimination
patients often immunosuppressed from disease/medications

design_impact.persistence: short-to-medium term may be enough (immune reset)
design_impact.CAAR_option: if specific autoantibody target known (Dsg3, MuSK) → CAAR design
design_impact.BCMA_vs_CD19: BCMA for long-lived plasma cells; CD19 for B cells; combination for complete depletion
design_impact.dose: lower CAR-T dose than oncology (10^6-10^7 range)
design_impact.safety: mRNA-CAR or lower dose preferred for safety

key_challenges: infection risk from B cell aplasia

sources.source_title: Anti-BCMA CAR T-cell therapy for refractory systemic lupus erythematosus

characteristics: goal is immune suppression, not killing
CAR-Treg approach: suppress allo-reactive T and B cells
must maintain stable Treg phenotype in inflammatory environment

design_impact.cell_chassis: Treg ONLY — conventional T cells would worsen GvHD
design_impact.treg_stability: FoxP3 OE mandatory; Helios OE recommended; TNFR2 costim preferred
design_impact.forbidden_elements: NO pro-inflammatory payloads (IL-12, IL-18, c-Jun, BATF)
design_impact.costim: 4-1BB acceptable but monitor Treg stability; ICOS under investigation

sources.source_title: Alloantigen-specific regulatory T cells generated with a chimeric antigen receptor

characteristics: target is pathogen-encoded antigen on infected host cells — zero normal-tissue expression
target cells may be anucleate (e.g. iRBC) → T cell cytotoxic pathway may be incompatible
acute infections impose tight therapeutic windows (hours–days)
pathogen antigens may exhibit antigenic variation (e.g. PfEMP1 var switching)
natural clearance mechanisms (phagocytosis, complement) already exist — CAR augments them
manufacturing time of ex-vivo products may exceed disease window

design_impact.cell_chassis: macrophage if target cell is anucleate or phagocytosis is native clearance; NK as alternative for ADCC-competent targets
design_impact.delivery: in_vivo_LNP_mRNA strongly preferred for acute infections (window <168h); ex_vivo acceptable for chronic infections (HIV, HBV)
design_impact.safety: pathogen-only antigen → on-target/off-tumor risk minimal; safety switch optional unless chronic expression
design_impact.costim: FcRg (phagocytic signaling) for macrophage; 4-1BB for T/NK
design_impact.armor: usually not needed — no immunosuppressive TME in blood-stage infections
design_impact.binder: broad-spectrum binder required if pathogen exhibits antigenic variation; VHH preferred over scFv for stability in harsh conditions
design_impact.multi_target: OR-gate for variable antigens with conserved + variable epitope coverage
design_impact.persistence: transient expression (mRNA) may be sufficient for acute; stable for chronic

key_challenges: anucleate target cells incompatible with T cell killing
acute therapeutic window precludes ex-vivo manufacturing
limited clinical precedent for CAR in infectious disease
pathogen biology poorly represented in CAR knowledge bases
references: Maldini et al. Nat Rev Immunol 18:605 (2018)

sources.source_title: Broadly inhibitory antibodies to severe malaria virulence proteins
CAR T cells and infectious diseases

D2_antigen_properties._note: Antigen structural and expression properties that directly determine hinge length, binder format, safety strategy, and multi-targeting need.

epitope_distance_lt_5nm.reason: Proximal epitope; short hinge provides optimal synapse formation

epitope_distance_5_10nm.reason: Intermediate distance; medium spacer maintains accessibility

epitope_distance_gt_10nm.reason: Distal epitope; long spacer prevents steric hindrance

CD19.heterogeneity: low — >95% positive in B-ALL, >90% in DLBCL
CD19.multi_target_rationale: CD19/CD22 tandem reduces antigen-loss relapse from 45% to <10%
CD19.on_target_off_tumor: B cell aplasia (manageable with IgG replacement)

sources.source_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
Hypogammaglobulinemia after Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy: characteristics, management, and future directions

BCMA.tumor_normal_ratio: ~2x vs plasma cells; infinite vs non-plasma
BCMA.heterogeneity: moderate — can be shed by gamma-secretase (soluble BCMA as decoy)

sources.source_title: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel, a B-cell maturation antigen-directed CAR-T cell therapy in patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma
Humoral immune reconstitution after anti-BCMA CAR T-cell therapy in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma
A Phase I Trial of Regional Mesothelin-Targeted CAR T-cell Therapy in Patients with Malignant Pleural Disease, in Combination with Pembrolizumab

HER2.tumor_normal_ratio: 2000x (breast 3+) but cardiac expression is FATAL risk
HER2.on_target_off_tumor: CRITICAL — cardiac myocyte expression → fatal CRS reported (NCI 2010)

sources.source_title: Case report of a serious adverse event following the administration of T cells transduced with a chimeric antigen receptor recognizing ERBB2
Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2-Specific Chimeric Antigen Receptor-Modified T Cells for the Immunotherapy of HER2-Positive Sarcoma

CLDN18_2.tumor_normal_ratio: 3-6x but isoform-specific antibody distinguishes CLDN18.2 from CLDN18.1
CLDN18_2.special_note: very membrane-proximal epitope → short hinge is ESSENTIAL; longer spacers abolish function

CD123.on_target_off_tumor: HIGH — shared with normal HSCs → myeloablation risk

sources.source_title: CD123 CAR T cells for the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome

CD22.antigen_loss_frequency: 10-15% (CD22 density modulation more common than complete loss)
CD22.multi_target_rationale: Primary salvage target after CD19 CAR-T failure

GD2.on_target_off_tumor: moderate (neurotoxic pain syndrome from peripheral nerve expression)
GD2.special_note: GD2 is a glycolipid, not a protein — atypical epitope

PfEMP1_CIDRa1.full_name: P. falciparum Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 1 — CIDRα1 domain
PfEMP1_CIDRa1.epitope_location: knob protrusion (30-50 nm above iRBC surface)
PfEMP1_CIDRa1.expression_window_description: trophozoite/schizont stages only — ~67% of 48h intraerythrocytic cycle

antigenic_variation.mechanism: mutually exclusive var gene transcriptional switching
antigenic_variation.conserved_epitope: EPCR-binding site (3 residues structurally conserved across 885 CIDRa1 sequences)

PfEMP1_CIDRa1.on_target_off_tumor: none (pathogen-encoded antigen — zero normal tissue expression)
PfEMP1_CIDRa1.heterogeneity: high (60 var gene variants; CIDRa1 subtypes 1.1–1.8)
PfEMP1_CIDRa1.antigen_loss_frequency: medium (var gene switching can alter CIDRa1 subtype)
binder_format_options: VHH (preferred — better thermal stability, single-domain, future development)
PfEMP1_CIDRa1.multi_target_rationale: OR-gate recommended: anti-CIDRa1.4-1.8 + anti-CIDRa1.1 for full coverage

sources.source_title: Broadly inhibitory antibodies to severe malaria virulence proteins
Structural conservation despite huge sequence diversity allows EPCR binding by PfEMP1

D3_delivery_modality._note: Ex-vivo vs in-vivo CAR delivery decision. Affects manufacturing, vector, and safety profile.

ex_vivo_autologous.description: Patient's own T/NK cells collected, transduced ex vivo, expanded, re-infused
ex_vivo_autologous.when: default for all current approved products

sources.source_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia

ex_vivo_allogeneic.description: Healthy donor T cells, gene-edited to remove TCR/MHC, banked and used off-the-shelf
ex_vivo_allogeneic.when: patient too sick for autologous; standardized product needed; cost reduction

sources.source_title: UCART19 study in relapsed/refractory CD19+ leukemia and lymphoma

in_vivo_LNP_mRNA.description: Lipid nanoparticle encapsulating CAR mRNA, targeting T cells in vivo via surface ligand (CD5 scFv)
in_vivo_LNP_mRNA.when: transient expression sufficient; repeated dosing acceptable; avoid manufacturing entirely

sources.source_title: CAR T cells produced in vivo to treat cardiac injury

in_vivo_viral.description: Viral vector (LV or fusogenic) injected IV to transduce T cells in vivo
in_vivo_viral.when: stable integration needed without ex-vivo step
disadvantages: insertional mutagenesis risk in non-target cells

in_vivo_LNP_mRNA_macrophage.description: Mannosylated LNP delivering CAR mRNA to macrophages via CD206 mannose receptor, targeting splenic red-pulp and hepatic Kupffer cells in situ
in_vivo_LNP_mRNA_macrophage.when: macrophage chassis selected; acute therapeutic window (<168h); need immediate effector function without ex-vivo manufacturing
advantages: onset <1h post-injection — compatible with acute infection windows
targets tissue-resident macrophages at natural pathogen clearance sites (spleen, liver)
no ex-vivo manufacturing — single IV injection
ManC-LNP achieves >80% macrophage specificity via CD206 targeting
disadvantages: transient only — requires repeat dosing for sustained effect
limited to macrophage lineage (not for T or NK cell programming)
hepatic first-pass may reduce splenic delivery efficiency

sources.source_title: Intraperitoneal programming of tailored CAR macrophages via mRNA-LNP

decision_rules.if: need immediate treatment AND allogeneic elements available
safety-critical target (HER2, shared antigen) AND transient expression acceptable
autoimmune AND low burden AND repeated dosing model

decision_rules.reason: acute window precludes ex-vivo manufacturing; ManC-LNP targets tissue macrophages in situ
acute window precludes ex-vivo manufacturing; CD5-LNP targets T cells in vivo
chronic infections allow ex-vivo manufacturing time; in-vivo also viable

D4_cell_chassis._note: Which immune cell type to engineer with CAR. Determines signaling, manufacturing, and clinical outcome.

alpha_beta_T.full_name: Conventional αβ T cells (CD4+ and CD8+)
disadvantages: variable quality from patient to patient
alpha_beta_T.manufacturing: apheresis → activation (anti-CD3/CD28 beads) → transduction → expansion (10-14 days)
alpha_beta_T.persistence: months to years (4-1BB); weeks to months (CD28)

sources.source_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma

disadvantages: lower peak cytotoxicity than T cells

sources.source_title: Use of CAR-Transduced Natural Killer Cells in CD19-Positive Lymphoid Tumors

NK.manufacturing: isolation → IL-15/IL-21 expansion → transduction → feeder cell expansion (14-21 days)

sources.source_title: CAR γδ T cells for cancer immunotherapy. Is the field more yellow than green?

advantages: phagocytose large tumor masses and anucleate infected cells
antigen presentation to recruit T cells
natural clearance mechanism for iRBCs in spleen/liver — CAR augments existing biology

in_vivo_mRNA_LNP.description: ManC-LNP IV injection → CD206-mediated uptake by splenic red-pulp macrophages and hepatic Kupffer cells → in situ CAR expression
in_vivo_mRNA_LNP.best_for: acute infections with tight therapeutic windows

macrophage.manufacturing: monocyte isolation → adenoviral transduction → maturation → infusion

sources.source_title: CAR-macrophage therapy for HER2-overexpressing advanced solid tumors: a phase 1 trial

Treg.forbidden_reason: Pro-inflammatory payloads destabilize FoxP3 expression → Treg becomes pathogenic effector T cell
Treg.manufacturing: CD4+CD25hi sorting → anti-CD3/CD28 + rapamycin expansion → transduction → expansion (14-21 days)

sources.source_title: Alloantigen-specific regulatory T cells generated with a chimeric antigen receptor

DC.concept: DC equipped with CAR captures tumor antigen → processes and presents via MHC → activates endogenous anti-tumor T cells

advantages: gene editing at clonal level (100% efficiency)

sources.source_title: FT596 off-the-shelf, iPSC-derived CAR NK cell program interim phase 1 data

NKT_iNKT.full_name: Invariant Natural Killer T cells (iNKT, Vα24-Jα18/Vβ11)
advantages: secrete both Th1 and Th2 cytokines
disadvantages: extremely low frequency (0.01-0.1% of blood T cells)
NKT_iNKT.manufacturing: iNKT sorting → alpha-GalCer + IL-2 expansion → transduction → expansion

sources.source_title: GD2 Specific CAR and Interleukin-15 Expressing Autologous NKT Cells to Treat Children With Neuroblastoma

malaria_cerebral.note: iRBC is anucleate — T cell caspase pathway incompatible; phagocytosis is natural clearance mechanism

malaria_severe.note: iRBC is anucleate — T cell caspase pathway incompatible

HIV.note: HIV-infected CD4+ T cells are nucleated; standard cytotoxic killing applies

CMV_reactivation.note: standard virus-specific T cell approach; nucleated target cells

D5_immune_microenvironment._note: Activation vs suppression balance in the target tissue. Determines armor, KO, and engineering requirements.

sources.source_title: Engineering strategies to overcome the current roadblocks in CAR T cell therapy

autoimmune_tissue.description: Inflamed tissue with pathogenic immune cells — CAR-T depletes the pathogenic cells
key_factors: cytokine storm potential at lower levels than oncology
autoimmune_tissue.countermeasures_needed: dose control; low lymphodepletion; monitor for CRS

suppression_to_element_map._note: Maps suppressive factor → library element IDs that counteract it

Chemokine Receptor CCR2b Enhanced Anti-tumor Function of Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cells Targeting Mesothelin in a Non-small-cell Lung Carcinoma Model

D6_vector_integration._note: Vector choice determines payload capacity, integration safety, manufacturing, and cost.

lentiviral_LV.integration_pattern: semi-random, favors body of active genes

sources.source_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia

gamma_retroviral_RV.integration_pattern: near transcription start sites and CpG islands (higher risk)
gamma_retroviral_RV.insertional_oncogenesis_risk: low but higher than LV (2 cases in X-SCID trials)

sources.source_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma

sleeping_beauty_SB.integration_pattern: TA dinucleotides (relatively random, less biased than RV)
sleeping_beauty_SB.insertional_oncogenesis_risk: low (random TA sites, no promoter bias)
sleeping_beauty_SB.delivery_method: electroporation of SB100X mRNA + transposon plasmid
advantages: no viral production needed (dramatic cost reduction)
disadvantages: less clinical data than viral vectors

sources.source_title: Phase I trials using Sleeping Beauty to generate CD19-specific CAR T cells

piggyBac_PB.delivery_method: electroporation of PB transposase mRNA + transposon plasmid
disadvantages: TTAA integration bias includes some oncogene-adjacent sites
piggyBac_PB.caution: PiggyBac integration near MYC locus reported in some studies → ongoing safety evaluation

mRNA_LNP.payload_capacity_kb: unlimited (mRNA length not limited by vector)
disadvantages: lower peak expression than integrating vectors

sources.source_title: CAR T cells produced in vivo to treat cardiac injury

AAV_TRAC_knockin.full_name: AAV6-mediated HDR knock-in at TRAC locus
AAV_TRAC_knockin.integration_pattern: site-specific at TRAC locus (when combined with CRISPR guide)
best_for: uniform CAR expression from endogenous TCR promoter
allogeneic CAR (simultaneous TCR KO + CAR KI)
advantages: physiological CAR expression level (reduced tonic signaling)

decision_rules.if: cost is critical AND non-viral acceptable

payload_capacity_check._note: Estimate construct size and recommend vector
payload_capacity_check.logic_gate_dual_vector: ~10-14kb total → dual LV or single SB/PB

D7_safety_framework._note: Risk assessment and mitigation strategy for every CAR design.

crs_risk_model.description: Cytokine Release Syndrome risk factors and mitigation

sources.source_title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma
Axicabtagene Ciloleucel CAR T-Cell Therapy in Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma

grade_management.grade_4: ICU, tocilizumab + high-dose steroids, consider siltuximab

design_mitigation.use_4_1BB: reduces CRS incidence vs CD28 (Kymriah 47% grade 3+ vs Yescarta 13%)
design_mitigation.CD3z_1XX_variant: single-ITAM CD3z reduces tonic signaling and may reduce CRS

neurotoxicity_ICANS.mechanism: endothelial activation → BBB disruption → cytokine entry into CNS

mitigation.clinical: monitor ICE score, dexamethasone for grade ≥2

sources.source_title: Complications after CD19+ CAR T-Cell Therapy

on_target_off_tumor.description: CAR kills normal cells expressing the same antigen at low levels

low.clinical_management: IgG replacement for B cell aplasia

A Phase I Trial of Regional Mesothelin-Targeted CAR T-cell Therapy in Patients with Malignant Pleural Disease, in Combination with Pembrolizumab

high.affinity_tuning: REQUIRED (lower affinity spares low-density normal cells)

sources.source_title: Case report of a serious adverse event following the administration of T cells transduced with a chimeric antigen receptor recognizing ERBB2

critical.additional: consider iCAR + activating CAR (NOT-gate) or regional delivery only

sources.source_title: Case report of a serious adverse event following the administration of T cells transduced with a chimeric antigen receptor recognizing ERBB2

tEGFR.speed: slower (hours to days, requires antibody infusion)

sources.source_title: UCART19 study in relapsed/refractory CD19+ leukemia and lymphoma

HSV_TK.limitation: only kills dividing cells; ganciclovir needed for CMV prophylaxis

mRNA_transient.best_for: inherently safe design for high-risk targets

antigen_loss_escape.frequency: 30-45% in CD19 B-ALL; 10-20% in BCMA MM

T_cell_exhaustion.frequency: major issue in all solid tumor CAR-T trials

poor_tumor_infiltration.design_approach: add homing receptor co-expression via 2A peptide

immunosuppressive_TME.design_approach: layer multiple countermeasures matched to specific TME profile

manufacturing_failure.mitigation: allogeneic off-the-shelf, iPSC-derived, or non-viral (SB) approach

D8_clinical_evidence._note: Landmark successes, instructive failures, and emerging field opinions that inform design choices.

landmark_successes.lesson: 4-1BB costimulation provides durable remission with lower CRS than CD28. Defined the 2G 4-1BB-CD3z architecture as gold standard for persistence.
CD28 costimulation gives faster, higher peak expansion — better for aggressive lymphoma where speed matters. Higher CRS but manageable.
Biepitopic VHH binder (two VHH domains targeting different BCMA epitopes) achieved 98% ORR — binder format and avidity matter enormously.
landmark_successes.design_principle: binder format (VHH, biepitopic, tandem) can be more impactful than signaling domain choice

landmark_successes.lesson: 73% ORR with NO CRS, NO ICANS, NO GvHD. mbIL-15 armor + iCasp9 safety switch. Proof that NK cells are viable CAR chassis.
landmark_successes.design_principle: CAR-NK = lower toxicity profile; mbIL-15 solves NK persistence

landmark_successes.lesson: 3/3 SLE patients achieved drug-free remission. Same CAR backbone as myeloma. Opens entire autoimmune field.
landmark_successes.design_principle: oncology CAR constructs can be repurposed for autoimmune with dose/protocol modifications

instructive_failures.what_happened: Patient received 10^10 CAR-T cells at first dose. Massive CRS within 15 minutes from CAR-T recognizing cardiac HER2 (1000 molecules/cell). Patient died within 5 days.
instructive_failures.lesson: On-target/off-tumor toxicity is FATAL for shared antigens. Dose escalation (start 10^7, not 10^10) is mandatory. Safety switch and/or affinity tuning required.
instructive_failures.reference: Morgan et al. Mol Ther 2010; PMID:20354568

sources.source_title: Morgan et al. Mol Ther 2010

instructive_failures.what_happened: Affinity-enhanced TCR cross-reacted with cardiac titin protein (similar peptide). 2 fatal cardiac events.
instructive_failures.lesson: Affinity enhancement can create off-target cross-reactivity. Epitope/cross-reactivity screening is critical, especially for affinity-matured binders.

sources.source_title: Linette et al. Blood 2013

instructive_failures.trial: CD19 CAR-T antigen loss relapse (multiple centers)
instructive_failures.what_happened: 30-45% of B-ALL patients relapsed with CD19-negative disease after initial complete remission.
instructive_failures.lesson: Single-target CAR selects for antigen-negative escape variants. Multi-targeting (tandem, sequential, OR-gate) is essential for durable remission.
instructive_failures.reference: Orlando et al. Nat Med 2018; PMID:30275568

sources.source_title: Orlando et al. Nat Med 2018

instructive_failures.what_happened: Majority of solid tumor CAR-T trials showed <20% response rate despite promising preclinical data.
instructive_failures.lesson: Standard 2G CAR is insufficient for solid tumors. Need multi-layered engineering: homing + anti-exhaustion + TME remodeling + checkpoint resistance.
instructive_failures.reference: Rafiq et al. Nat Rev Clin Oncol 2020; PMID:31900437

sources.source_title: Rafiq et al. Nat Rev Clin Oncol 2020

instructive_failures.what_happened: 5 patient deaths from fatal cerebral edema. Construct used CD28 costim + fludarabine/cyclophosphamide lymphodepletion.
instructive_failures.lesson: CD28 costim + aggressive lymphodepletion = excessive expansion and neurotoxicity. Led to Breyanzi redesign with 4-1BB and defined CD4/CD8 composition.

design_impact.costim_choice: 4-1BB preferred when neurotoxicity risk is concern

instructive_failures.reference: FDA clinical hold 2016; Turtle et al. JCI 2016

sources.source_title: FDA clinical hold 2016

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: Injectable vaccine presenting CAR target antigen in amphiphile form → boosts CAR-T expansion in vivo after infusion. Tested with mesothelin CAR-T.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.design_impact: may eliminate need for high initial CAR-T dose

sources.source_title: Ma et al. Science 2019

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.concept: dual checkpoint KO (PD-1 + TIGIT or PD-1 + LAG-3)
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: Combined knockout of two inhibitory receptors for additive anti-exhaustion in solid tumors.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.design_impact: dual KO better than single for heavily suppressed TME

sources.source_title: Chauvin et al. JCI 2020

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: Multi-input logic (AND, OR, NOT gates) for safer solid tumor targeting. SynNotch most clinically advanced.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.design_impact: enables safe targeting of antigens with normal tissue expression
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.reference: Roybal et al. Cell 2016; Hyrenius-Wittsten et al. Science 2021

sources.source_title: Roybal et al. Cell 2016

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: KO of DNA methyltransferases prevents exhaustion-associated epigenetic silencing. Maintains stem-like T cell phenotype.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.caution: TET2 KO showed clonal expansion in one patient (N=1) — monitor carefully
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.reference: Prinzing et al. Sci Immunol 2021; Fraietta et al. Nature 2018

sources.source_title: Prinzing et al. Sci Immunol 2021

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: Direct injection of T-cell-targeted LNP carrying CAR mRNA → CAR-T generated in vivo without manufacturing.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.design_impact: eliminates manufacturing bottleneck; enables repeated dosing

sources.source_title: Rurik et al. Science 2022

emerging_concepts_2024_2025.description: Combining membrane-bound IL-15 and IL-21 for NK persistence and stem-like programming.
Base editors (BE3, ABE8e) instead of DSB-CRISPR for TCR/B2M KO. Avoids translocation risk from dual DSBs.
Expanding from SLE to myasthenia gravis, pemphigus, systemic sclerosis, type 1 diabetes. Both CD19 and BCMA targets.
emerging_concepts_2024_2025.clinical_stage: Phase I/II (multiple centers in Germany, China, US)

field_consensus_2025.solid_tumor: Standard 2G CAR is insufficient. Minimum viable product = armored 4G with anti-exhaustion + homing + TME countermeasure.
field_consensus_2025.hematologic: CD19 CAR-T is established. Next frontier = multi-targeting (CD19/CD22), AML (safety-critical), and moving to first-line.
field_consensus_2025.manufacturing: Autologous is bottleneck. Allogeneic (CRISPR-edited) and iPSC-derived are the path to scalability.
field_consensus_2025.safety: CRS is manageable. On-target/off-tumor remains the biggest safety concern for new targets. Logic gates are the solution.
field_consensus_2025.autoimmune: Most exciting new application. Lower risk than oncology (lower burden). BCMA CAR-T for SLE is paradigm-shifting.
field_consensus_2025.in_vivo: In-vivo CAR (LNP-mRNA) is 5-10 years from clinical reality but could be transformative.

D9_patient_fitness_and_cmc._note: Patient history and T-cell fitness dictating manufacturing and engineering rescue.

healthy_donor.description: Allogeneic source, high stemness, no prior chemo.
healthy_donor.engineering_impact: Requires TCR KO and HLA evasion. No exhaustion rescue needed.

sources.source_title: A Safety and Efficacy Study Evaluating UCART19 in Patients with Relapsed or Refractory CD19+ Leukemia and Lymphoma

moderate_prior_therapy.description: Standard autologous patient, 1-2 prior lines of therapy.
moderate_prior_therapy.cmc_recommendation: IL-7 + IL-15 to preserve Tscm (stem cell memory) phenotype.

sources.source_title: Closely related T-memory stem cells correlate with in vivo expansion of CAR.CD19-T cells and are preserved by IL-7 and IL-15

heavily_pretreated.description: 3+ prior lines, fludarabine/cyclophosphamide exposed, severe lymphopenia.
heavily_pretreated.cmc_recommendation: FasT CAR-T (24-48h manufacturing) to avoid ex-vivo exhaustion, OR IL-15/IL-21 cocktail.
heavily_pretreated.engineering_impact: CRITICAL: Require epigenetic rescue (DNMT3A-KO, TET2-KO) or c-Jun OE to restore function.

rapid_progression.engineering_impact: Prioritize speed over complex multi-gene edits.

sources.source_title: Engineering strategies to overcome the current roadblocks in CAR T cell therapy

evidence_confidence_model._note: Evidence weighting for rules, target pairs, and design recommendations.

approved_human.definition: Supported by approved product or label-level human evidence

clinical_human.definition: Supported by prospective clinical trial or strong clinical case series

preclinical_in_vitro.definition: Supported by in vitro or ex vivo data only

toxicity_tolerance_model._note: Separates expression ratio from clinical tolerability of normal-cell depletion.

acceptable_depletion.definition: Normal target-positive cells can be depleted with standard supportive care.
acceptable_depletion.design_bias: Safety switch recommended but not mandatory; logic gate usually not required.

manageable_toxicity.definition: Normal tissue injury is possible but can be mitigated by route, dose, or affinity tuning.
manageable_toxicity.design_bias: Safety switch recommended; affinity tuning and dose escalation helpful.

high_risk_shared_antigen.definition: Normal hematopoietic or epithelial expression creates serious toxicity risk.
high_risk_shared_antigen.design_bias: Safety switch mandatory; consider logic gate, transient expression, or alternative chassis.

vital_organ_expression.definition: Low-level expression in heart, lung, CNS, or other essential tissue can be catastrophic.
vital_organ_expression.design_bias: Logic gate or transient design strongly preferred; safety switch mandatory.

tumor_restricted.definition: Little or no meaningful adult normal tissue expression.

pathogen_only.definition: Antigen encoded by pathogen genome; zero expression on uninfected human cells.
pathogen_only.design_bias: On-target/off-tumor risk is zero. Safety switch optional. Standard or minimal CAR architecture.

core_assembly_rules._note: Biological reasoning rules for core CAR chain assembly: SP, Hinge, TM, Costim, Activation. Each rule produces use/dont_use rationale.

rules.rationale: CD8α SP yields highest surface expression in FDA-approved CARs (Tisa-cel, Axi-cel, Liso-cel). 21aa leader — efficient ER translocation for scFv-based constructs.
IgKappa SP provides better secretory pathway processing for single-domain (VHH/nanobody) binders due to matched Ig-fold translocation.
GM-CSF SP is optimized for myeloid cell surface expression. CD8α SP has reduced efficiency in macrophage ER.

dont_use_general.reason: IL-2 SP promotes secretion over membrane retention — inappropriate for CAR surface display.
Gaussia SP is a secretory signal — poor surface retention for transmembrane constructs.
GPI signal is a C-terminal anchor — incompatible with N-terminal CAR topology.

hinge_tm_pairing._note: Hinge + TM form a coupled unit. Mismatched pairings increase tonic signaling and reduce stability.

CD8a_Short+CD8a_TM.rationale: CD8α hinge + CD8α TM: minimal homodimerization-driven tonic signaling. Proven in Tisa-cel (FDA-approved). Best for 4-1BB costimulation.

CD28_Medium+CD28_TM.rationale: CD28 hinge + CD28 TM: matched pair from Axi-cel/Yescarta. CD28 TM dimerization domain is intrinsic — pairs best with CD28 costim for rapid activation kinetics.

IgG4_SPLE_Long+CD8a_TM.rationale: Long IgG4 spacer (S228P L235E) reaches distal epitopes (>10nm). CD8α TM reduces tonic signaling from IgG4 dimerization. CH2/CH3 Fc-null mutations REQUIRED to prevent off-target FcR binding.

IgG4_SPLE_Long+CD28_TM.rationale: AVOID: IgG4 long hinge + CD28 TM creates excessive tonic signaling from dual dimerization interfaces. Leads to premature exhaustion.

hinge_selection._note: Primary determinant is antigen epitope-to-membrane distance.

rules.rationale: Membrane-proximal epitope (<5nm) → short hinge. Long spacers would push the immune synapse beyond effective signaling distance, reducing cytotoxicity by 3-10x.

reject_rationale.IgG4_SPLE_Long: Excessive inter-membrane distance disrupts immune synapse geometry for proximal targets.
reject_rationale.IgD_Hinge: Experimental only; no clinical validation for proximal epitopes.

sources.source_title: Hinge domain engineering for optimal CAR-T function

rules.rationale: Medium-distance epitope (5-10nm) → medium hinge (CD28). Short hinge would be insufficient to bridge the synapse gap.

reject_rationale.CD8a_Short: Insufficient reach for medium-distance epitopes — reduced T cell activation.

rules.rationale: Distal epitope (>10nm) requires long spacer. IgG4 S228P/L235E prevents FcγR binding while providing ~23nm reach. CH2 deletion variant also acceptable.

reject_rationale.CD8a_Short: Cannot reach distal epitopes — synapse fails to form.

tm_selection._note: TM selection is coupled to hinge choice and chassis type.

rules.rationale: CD8α hinge pairs with CD8α TM — matched pair minimizes tonic signaling. CD28 TM with CD8α hinge creates mismatched dimerization domains.
CD28 hinge + CD28 TM: structurally coupled in the native CD28 protein. Matched pair validated in Axi-cel, Brexu-cel (FDA-approved).
IgG4 long hinge already provides dimerization — adding CD28 TM dimerization creates excessive tonic signaling. CD8α TM keeps signaling controlled.
NKG2D TM is native to NK cells — enables DAP10/DAP12 adaptor association. CD8α/CD28 TM functional but NKG2D is NK-optimized.
CD8α TM provides stable surface expression on macrophages. FcγRI TM+cyto is an alternative for full phagocytic signaling.

costim_selection._note: Costimulatory domain selection is driven by disease biology, chassis type, and persistence requirements.

rules.rationale: Solid tumors require persistent T cells that resist exhaustion. 4-1BB activates TRAF1/2 → NF-κB, promotes mitochondrial biogenesis and fatty acid oxidation (FAO), driving Tcm differentiation. This yields longer persistence (months vs weeks). CD28 drives glycolysis → Teff bias → rapid exhaustion in chronic antigen exposure.

reject_rationale.CD28_cyto: CD28 costimulation activates PI3K/Akt/mTOR → glycolytic Teff phenotype. In solid tumor TME with chronic antigen, this causes rapid T cell exhaustion within 1-2 weeks. Also associated with higher CRS grade.

sources.source_title: Long et al. 4-1BB costimulation ameliorates T cell exhaustion
Ying et al. Parallel comparison of 4-1BB vs CD28 in CAR-T

rules.conditional_note: Exception: if indication requires rapid tumor clearance (e.g. high-burden liquid tumor), CD28 may be preferred for faster kinetics.

rules.rationale: 4-1BB is default for liquid tumors (used in Tisa-cel, Liso-cel). Provides durable responses. CD28 is a valid alternative (Axi-cel) when rapid clearance is priority.
rules.alternative_rationale: CD28 produces faster peak expansion and tumor clearance, beneficial in high-burden B-ALL/DLBCL. Trade-off: shorter persistence, higher CRS.

rules.rationale: Autoimmune CAR (CD19-targeted B cell depletion) requires controlled, sustained depletion — not explosive expansion. 4-1BB provides lower CRS and longer persistence for complete B cell ablation. CD28's rapid kinetics increase CRS risk in non-oncology patients with lower tolerance.
ICOS costimulation maintains FoxP3+ Treg stability and suppressive function. CD28 destabilizes Tregs toward Teff conversion. 4-1BB may impair suppressive capacity via excessive NF-κB activation.

reject_rationale.CD28_cyto: Drives Treg-to-Teff conversion — catastrophic for tolerance induction.
reject_rationale.4-1BB_cyto: Excessive NF-κB may destabilize FoxP3 program; preclinical data inconsistent.

rules.alternative_rationale: Some groups report stable Treg function with 4-1BB. Use only with FoxP3 OE and IL-2 supplementation.

rules.rationale: 2B4 (CD244) is a native NK cell costimulatory receptor — signals through SAP/Fyn pathway optimized for NK cytotoxicity. DAP10 is an alternative for NKG2D-based CARs.

reject_rationale.CD28_cyto: CD28 is not natively expressed on NK cells — signaling pathway is suboptimal.

rules.rationale: FcRγ provides phagocytic ITAM signaling native to macrophages. TIR domains (MyD88/TLR2) are alternatives for inflammatory polarization. T cell costim domains (4-1BB, CD28) are functionally incompatible with macrophage signaling.

reject_rationale.4-1BB_cyto: TRAF signaling pathway not functional in macrophage context.
reject_rationale.CD28_cyto: PI3K/Akt pathway from CD28 does not drive phagocytosis.

rules.rationale: AML targets (CD123, CD33) have significant normal-tissue expression — lower CRS from 4-1BB is critical for safety. CD28's rapid expansion increases CRS risk in the already-vulnerable AML patient population.

activation_selection._note: Primary signaling domain — nearly always CD3ζ 3×ITAM for T cells. Alternatives for specific chassis or attenuation.

rules.rationale: CD3ζ 3×ITAM is the universal standard for CAR-T activation. All FDA-approved CARs use full CD3ζ. Provides robust TCR-like Signal 1.
Standard CD3ζ 3×ITAM remains default. CD3ζ 1×ITAM (attenuated) is experimental alternative — reduces tonic signaling but may compromise effector function.
rules.alternative_rationale: 1×ITAM variant reduces exhaustion from chronic signaling in solid TME. Trade-off: lower peak cytotoxicity. Use only when anti-exhaustion engineering (c-Jun OE) is also included.

rules.rationale: FcRγ 1×ITAM provides phagocytic signaling in macrophages. CD3ζ ITAM signaling is T cell-specific and does not efficiently trigger phagocytosis.

IL-2Rβ cytoplasmic domain provides JAK-STAT5 signal (Signal 3) in addition to CD3ζ (Signal 1) + costim (Signal 2). Enables antigen-dependent T cell proliferation without exogenous cytokines. Used in tandem with CD3ζ, not as replacement.
